title: React media-gallery SPA for the dufs cloud
objective: Give the self-hosted cloud a rich web UI at its existing URL: a React SPA served by dufs that shows thumbnails, opens images in a lightbox, plays videos inline, and supports upload, download, delete, and editing text files — all behind the existing dufs auth, without breaking the KeePass sync.
acceptance_criteria: The SPA is served same-origin by dufs (render-spa); it lists directories via WebDAV PROPFIND and streams files via GET (Range for video), so dufs basic-auth covers every request.
Grid shows generated thumbnails (images via ImageMagick, video posters via ffmpeg) and falls back to an icon when a thumbnail is missing.
Images open in a lightbox (zoom + keyboard/next/prev); videos play inline; files can be uploaded, downloaded, and deleted (confirm-guarded); .txt/.md files can be edited and saved.
Enabling render-spa does not break Phase-1 KeePass file GET/PUT/?hash.
cloud_gallery builds, lints (tsc + eslint strictTypeChecked), and its vitest suite passes with coverage above the project thresholds.
out_of_scope: Server-side rendering / Next.js (dufs is a static file server)
Running the live deploy in this session (interactive + outward-facing)
Fixing the host's broken ffmpeg (video posters skipped until then)
verifier: de-risk against the real dufs binary (render-spa + PROPFIND/GET/PUT/DELETE/?hash); pnpm build + pnpm lint + pnpm coverage (38 tests, >90%); generate_thumbnails.sh functional test; local dufs --render-spa serving check (shell/asset/thumbnail/PROPFIND all 200); documented in docs/superpowers/evidence/cloud-gallery-2026-07-12.json
